figures.caption: Table 1: Upper bounds on the variance term in the bias-variance decomposition (25) for each method and Flow Matching subcase, with weight w(x, t) = ηt. For AM/AS, Y ∼ pbase, and for NSM, Y ∼ pdata ∝ pbaseer. Entries marked +∞ indicate that the integrand diverges at the time boundaries, precluding optimization of the path KL divergence.

Figure 1: Trade-offs between per-prompt diversity (DreamSim variance, x-axis) and quality metrics (ImageReward, CLIPScore, HPSv2; y-axis) for Stable Diffusion 1.5 fine-tuned with Adjoint Matching. Each point corresponds to a different combination of inference noise η ∈ {0, 1}, reward multiplier λ ∈ {1, 10, 103/2, 100}, and schedule (default DDIM or fine-tuning schedule).
Figure 2: Trade-offs between per-prompt diversity (DreamSim variance, x-axis) and quality metrics (yaxis) for Stable Diffusion 3 fine-tuned with Adjoint Matching.
Figure 3: Quality metrics for the base Stable Diffusion 3 model and models fine-tuned at σ2 0 = 1.5 and λ ∈ {102, 105/2, 103}, with inference at η ∈ {0, 1} and σ0 = {1, 1.5}.
